—We must motivate ourselves to give glory to God, and encourage ourselves to hope for mercy and grace from him. It is a good plan to fill all our free minutes with religious meditations, prayers, and praises. No time would then be a burden, nor should we waste our hours with frivolous conversation and empty pleasures, or by physical indulgences. We need desire no more to make us happy than to be blessed by the Lord. We ought to ask for spiritual blessings, not only for ourselves, but for others; not only, ‘The Lord bless me,’ but, ‘The Lord bless you’; thus showing our belief that there is enough for others as well as for us, and demonstrating our goodwill to others.
